picks.thesis: Bullish setup on FDX: Earnings Surprise (+25.5%) — 2026-02-28 — actual 5.25 vs est 4.18261; Guidance (+8.7%) — +8.7% FY EPS growth est — raised/in-line proxy; Put/Call Ratio Extreme (1.33 P/C) — P/C 1.33 — extreme fear, contrarian bullish; EPS Growth YoY (+17.3%); Revenue Growth YoY (+8.3%); Analyst Price Target (+16% to $393.83) — 26 analysts; P/E vs Sector (18.1) — 59% of sector median — discount; Free Cash Flow TTM ($894M) — positive trailing-12mo FCF.

drivers.text: Guidance (+8.7%) — +8.7% FY EPS growth est — raised/in-line proxy
Put/Call Ratio Extreme (1.33 P/C) — P/C 1.33 — extreme fear, contrarian bullish
P/E vs Sector (18.1) — 59% of sector median — discount
Free Cash Flow TTM ($894M) — positive trailing-12mo FCF

takeProfit.reason: No resistance far enough above to bank into — target $449.95 (+32.9%), the chain's 1σ expected move by expiry. That's where the option has gained real value; beyond it you're betting on an outlier.

cut.reason: Reduce/exit on a close below $311.41 — losing an ~8% stop (nearest support sits too far below to be a useful exit) breaks the bullish setup that drove the call (thesis invalidated). Cut before theta compounds the loss.

reasons.technical: round number $450.00 at $449.95 (+32.9%) — where prior advances have stalled and supply sits. RSI would likely be stretched (70+) into this level, so sell into strength rather than chase an extension.
reasons.fundamental: Roughly prices in the next 2-3 quarters of the growth the trade is paying for — beyond it you're betting on an outlier, not the thesis.
reasons.mechanical: Into a run like this, put/call usually compresses toward ~0.5 (peak greed) — it's 1.33 now: a contrarian cue to bank the move.
reasons.narrative: Sell into strength rather than wait for the catalyst to fully play out.

levels.prose: Into round number $450.00, where the move is largely priced in — sell into strength.
levels.proseAi: Sell into strength at the round number where prior advances stalled and supply sits, as RSI will be stretched and peak greediness is likely.

reasons.technical: the strike at $420.00 (+24.1%) — the first real overhead. Watch whether it breaks on volume or rejects.
reasons.mechanical: Confirm a break with 1.3x+ relative volume and rising call volume — a low-volume poke is a fakeout, so take the trim.

levels.watchFor: Breaks on 1.3x+ volume → ride toward $449.95 (+32.9%); stalls or RSI diverges → take the trim.
levels.proseAi: Take profit at the strike, confirming a break with volume or exiting on divergence.

reasons.technical: round number $400.00 at $400.00 (+18.2%) — the first real overhead. Watch whether it breaks on volume or rejects.
reasons.mechanical: Confirm a break with 1.3x+ relative volume and rising call volume — a low-volume poke is a fakeout, so take the trim.

levels.watchFor: Breaks on 1.3x+ volume → ride toward $420.00 (+24.1%); stalls or RSI diverges → take the trim.
levels.proseAi: Exit at the round number, confirming a break with volume or exiting on divergence.

reasons.technical: round number $350.00 at $350.00 (+3.4%) — the first real overhead. Watch whether it breaks on volume or rejects.
reasons.mechanical: Confirm a break with 1.3x+ relative volume and rising call volume — a low-volume poke is a fakeout, so take the trim.

levels.watchFor: Breaks on 1.3x+ volume → ride toward $400.00 (+18.2%); stalls or RSI diverges → take the trim.
levels.proseAi: Exit at the round number, confirming a break with volume or exiting on divergence.

reasons.technical: round number $330.00 at $330.00 (-2.5%) — the first crack below spot. Losing it on volume warns the cut is next; reduce rather than hope.
reasons.mechanical: Watch for a put-volume spike or fresh put open interest here — smart money hedging ahead of the break.

levels.watchFor: Loses it on volume → the cut at $311.41 is next; tighten up.
levels.proseAi: Reduce on volume loss below the round number, watching for put buying.

reasons.technical: A daily close below $311.41 (-8.0%) breaks the structure driving the call — the chart says the move is over. Don't hold a long call under broken support.
reasons.fundamental: A guidance cut or a macro risk-off rotation is what usually drives price here — exit, don't average down.
reasons.mechanical: A surge in put buying / put-call pushing back above ~1.25 confirms the turn.
reasons.narrative: A negative catalyst or a sector-narrative flip removes the reason for the trade — exit on the headline.

levels.prose: A close below round number $310.00 breaks the structure the trade is built on.
levels.proseAi: Cut the position if structure breaks below support on a catalyst.

triggers: Exit before earnings (2026-06-23): the post-report IV crush can erase a long premium even when the direction is right.
Time stop: don't carry into the final week before Jul 17 '26 — theta accelerates and the breakeven move gets harder each day.
Thesis check: if a higher-scoring setup appears or the original reason for the trade stops being true, rotate out regardless of price.
exitPlan.overviewAi: Scale out of this long call on strength, taking profits at key resistance levels and cutting losses if support breaks.

strategy.name: Moving-Average Pullback
strategy.blurb: In an uptrend, buy the pullback to the 20D/50D SMA — trend-followers defend these in a strong tape.

entryPlan.sizingRule: Enter 50% at the first signal (the first pullback to $328.34), then add 50% on a deeper pullback to $314.80. Never add below the $311.41 cut — that's where the thesis breaks.

tranches.label: ~3% pullback

reasons.technical: ~3% pullback at $328.34 (-3.0%) — the zone prior pullbacks have bounced from. Wait for price to come to it; a hold with RSI turning back up is the entry, not a falling knife.
reasons.fundamental: The fundamental edge behind the score (Earnings Surprise) doesn't change on a routine dip — weakness into this level is price, not a thesis break.
reasons.mechanical: Confirm with rising volume on the bounce and call flow / call OI building — a low-volume drift through $328.34 is the cue to wait for the next layer.
reasons.narrative: Enter while the catalyst behind the score is still in force — if the story flips before the fill, stand down.

tranches.prose: Your first add when price comes to the ~3% pullback.

tranches.label: ~7% pullback
tranches.trigger: Final piece only if it tags $314.80 and holds above the $311.41 cut

reasons.technical: ~7% pullback at $314.80 (-7.0%) — the last layer, just above the $311.41 cut. Below the cut the setup is broken; this is the deepest you add, not a hope buy.
reasons.fundamental: The fundamental edge behind the score (Earnings Surprise) doesn't change on a routine dip — weakness into this level is price, not a thesis break.
reasons.mechanical: Confirm with rising volume on the bounce and call flow / call OI building — a low-volume drift through $314.80 is the cue to wait for the next layer.
reasons.narrative: Enter while the catalyst behind the score is still in force — if the story flips before the fill, stand down.

tranches.prose: ~7% pullback — the deepest layer, just shy of the cut.

entryPlan.summary: Moving-Average Pullback — scale in over 2 tranches.

picks.analysis: FDX scores +18 — a Call recommendation. The bullish read is led by Fundamentals (+10), reinforced by Technicals (+5). The biggest contributors are Earnings Surprise (+2, +25.5%), Guidance (+2, +8.7%), Put/Call Ratio Extreme (+2, 1.33 P/C). Cutting the other way: 52-Week High/Low (-1, 98% of range). Within Industrials, the next-closest same-direction peer is LUNR at +13 — well below FDX's +18 (FDX leads on Fundamentals (+10 vs +6) and Mechanicals (+3 vs +1)), so FDX is the cleaner calls expression. The suggested calls sit ~24.1% OTM with 44d to expiry, delta 0.32, mid $19.65 — well inside the 5-30% OTM / Δ 0.20-0.40 / ≤$35 premium / standard-monthly criteria.
